skill.name: security
skill.description: Guides security validation, vulnerability scanning, key checks, and release readiness reviews for software projects.
skill.summary: Run structured security checks before commits and releases.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a command block and two weak-cryptography patterns. The command block is documented guidance for local security tools, while the weak-cryptography matches are false positives in descriptive security text.

low_findings.title: Documented External Security Commands
low_findings.description: The skill lists local commands for GPG, SSH, git signing, Bandit, pip-audit, Safety, and Semgrep. These are not auto-executed by the skill, but users should review commands before running them in a project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block is present and clearly invokes external tools. Confidence is high that the pattern exists, but the security impact is low because SKILL.md only documents commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings point to general security prose and an OWASP item about insecure deserialization. No weak cryptographic algorithm or implementation guidance is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text, not cryptographic code or algorithm recommendations. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak crypto usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for security scanning and key inspection. This requires user discretion because external tools can read local project files during scans.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in the documented command block. They are security tools, not hidden execution logic.

content.value_statement: Software teams need repeatable checks before code reaches users. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise security workflow for keys, dependencies, OWASP risks, and release readiness.
actual_capabilities: Guides GPG secret key and SSH agent validation.
Lists git signing key checks for development environments.
Provides commands for Bandit, pip-audit, Safety, and Semgrep scans.
Defines pre-commit and pre-release security checklist items.
Summarizes OWASP Top 10 review areas for application code.
limitations: Does not include executable workflow files beyond the main SKILL.md guidance.
Does not automatically run scanners or validate scan results.
Assumes the required security tools are installed in the project environment.
Provides general OWASP guidance, not legal, compliance, or penetration testing certification.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Security Check
use_cases.description: Run a quick checklist for secrets, dependency risk, and Python security findings before committing code.

use_cases.title: Release Readiness Review
use_cases.description: Confirm known vulnerabilities are addressed and document advisory needs before publishing a release.

use_cases.title: OWASP Risk Walkthrough
use_cases.description: Use the OWASP Top 10 prompts to review input validation, access control, data exposure, and logging.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Environment
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of local signing and key setup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security skill to help me validate my GPG key, SSH key, and git signing configuration. Explain each check before I run it.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Scan Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan scanner commands for a project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security skill to create a safe scan plan for this repository. Include dependency checks, Bandit, and Semgrep, but do not run commands until I approve them.

prompt_templates.title: Review Release Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security skill to review release readiness. Check unresolved vulnerabilities, dependency status, security advisory needs, and secure configuration risks.

prompt_templates.title: Map Findings to OWASP
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security skill to map these scan findings to OWASP Top 10 categories. Prioritize exploitable issues, explain impact, and suggest remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Help me run a pre-commit security check.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a short checklist covering secrets, dependencies, Bandit results, and scan failures that should block the commit.

output_examples.input: Review these vulnerability scan results before release.
output_examples.output: The assistant groups findings by severity, identifies unresolved release blockers, and recommends advisory or dependency update actions.

output_examples.input: Explain which OWASP risks apply to this authentication change.
output_examples.output: The assistant highlights authentication, access control, session management, input validation, and logging considerations.

best_practices: Review every command before execution and confirm it matches your repository layout.
Run dependency and static analysis scans before releases and after major dependency changes.
Treat secrets, signing keys, and scan reports as sensitive project information.
anti_patterns: Running scanner commands blindly without checking their scope or configuration.
Treating a clean scan as proof that the application is secure.
Ignoring dependency updates after a known vulnerability is reported.

faq.question: Does this skill run security tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and checklists. The user or agent must choose when to run each command.

faq.question: Which scanners does it mention?
faq.answer: It mentions Bandit, pip-audit, Safety, Semgrep, and gitleaks in its security checklist and command guidance.

faq.question: Can it validate GPG and SSH keys?
faq.answer: It can guide checks for GPG secret keys, SSH agent keys, and git signing configuration.

faq.question: Is this a penetration testing skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a lightweight security validation and review workflow, not a full penetration testing methodology.

faq.question: Does it cover OWASP Top 10 risks?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists OWASP Top 10 areas and provides short review reminders for each category.

faq.question: What should users verify before using it?
faq.answer: Users should verify tool availability, project paths, scanner configuration, and permission to inspect the target codebase.
